title: The datetime Module
subtitle: Work with dates, times, and time differences
learning_objectives: Get today's date and the current time
Create specific date and datetime objects
Calculate the difference between two dates
Format dates as readable strings with strftime

sections.heading: Getting Today's Date and Time
sections.content: The datetime module has a datetime class and a date class. Use date.today() for today's date, and datetime.now() for the full current date and time.
sections.code_example:
from datetime import date, datetime

# Today's date
today = date.today()
print(today)
print(type(today))

# Current date and time
now = datetime.now()
print(now)
print(now.year, now.month, now.day)
print(now.hour, now.minute, now.second)

sections.heading: Date Arithmetic and Formatting
sections.content: Subtract two dates to get a timedelta object that tells you how many days apart they are. Use strftime() to format a date as a readable string.
sections.code_example:
from datetime import date, timedelta

# Date arithmetic
birthday = date(2000, 6, 15)
today = date.today()
age_days = (today - birthday).days
print(f"Days since birthday: {age_days}")

# Add days to a date
future = today + timedelta(days=30)
print("30 days from today:", future)

# Format as readable string
formatted = today.strftime("%B %d, %Y")
print(formatted)  # e.g. May 03, 2026
sections.code_output:
Days since birthday: 9454 (varies)
30 days from today: 2026-06-02 (varies)
May 03, 2026 (varies)

exercises.title: Days Until New Year
exercises.prompt: Calculate how many days until January 1st, 2027. Print: 'Days until 2027: X'. (Note: this will vary depending on today's date.)
exercises.starter_code:
from datetime import date

today = date.today()
new_year = date(2027, 1, 1)

# Calculate the difference and print
exercises.solution:
from datetime import date

today = date.today()
new_year = date(2027, 1, 1)

days_left = (new_year - today).days
print("Days until 2027:", days_left)
hints: The .days attribute gives you the number of days

exercises.title: Format Today's Date
exercises.prompt: Print today's date in the format: 'Today is: Weekday, Month DD YYYY' — for example: 'Today is: Saturday, May 03 2026'. Use strftime with %A for weekday, %B for month name, %d for day, %Y for year.
exercises.starter_code:
from datetime import date

today = date.today()
# Format and print the date
exercises.solution:
from datetime import date

today = date.today()
formatted = today.strftime("%A, %B %d %Y")
print("Today is:", formatted)
hints: %A = full weekday, %B = full month name, %d = day, %Y = year

quiz.question: What does (date2 - date1).days give you?
options: The number of months between the dates
The number of days between the two dates
quiz.explanation: Subtracting two date objects gives a timedelta object. Accessing .days on the timedelta gives you the difference in days between the two dates.

quiz.question: What does strftime() do?
options: Parses a string into a date
Formats a date object as a readable string
quiz.explanation: strftime (string format time) converts a date or datetime object into a formatted string. You pass a format pattern like '%B %d, %Y' and get back a string like 'May 03, 2026'.

completion_message: You can now work with dates and times — very useful for real applications!
